title: aconitum napellus subsp. neomontanum
intro: Aconitum napellus subsp. neomontanum, also known as the Monk's Hood or Neomontane Monk's Hood, is a subspecies of the Aconitum napellus plant, which is a type of flowering plant in the Ranunculaceae family. It contains a range of bioactive compounds, including alkaloids and glycosides, which have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The plant has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including pain, inflammation, and fever, and is also believed to have antiviral, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. Aconitum napellus subsp. neomontanum can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tinctures, infusions, and ointments, but due to its toxic nature, it should only be used under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
